task: Type-3 DFIG external LVRT trip prototype design and offline waveform replay
prototype_scope: offline abstract trip_request/trip_latch prediction only; no PSCAD implementation and no BRK_DFIG connection

project_boundary_decision.Vs_ge_0p90: low-voltage event ends; reset timer only if trip_latch is not set

candidate_input.reason: existing Type-3 branch/collector-side voltage output used by duration sweep analysis
candidate_input.semantic_limits: candidate external POC voltage input only; final PSCAD control source, filtering, scaling, and wiring remain unconfirmed

input_semantic_limits: VIBR1_2 is replayed from saved PGB output, not from a live control wire.
The replay starts monitoring at the known fault start time 2.0 s to avoid startup transients being treated as LVRT events.
No conclusion is made about actual BRK_DFIG opening or Type-3 disconnection.

state_machine.event_start: if VIBR1_2 < 0.90 pu and no current event and trip_latch=0, latch event_start_time and Vs_min_latched
state_machine.immediate_trip: if Vs_min_latched <= 0.20 pu, set trip_request=1 and trip_latch=1
state_machine.duration_trip: if 0.20 < Vs_min_latched < 0.90 and elapsed >= tVRT(Vs_min_latched), set trip_request=1 and trip_latch=1
state_machine.event_reset: if VIBR1_2 >= 0.90 pu before trip_latch, clear low-voltage event state
state_machine.latch: once trip_latch=1, it does not reset automatically in the same simulation

timer_method: minimum-voltage latch within continuous low-voltage event
trip_latch_behavior: abstract latch only; no auto-reclose and no physical breaker command in PSCAD in this task
replay_status: all six scenario-local 3IBR.inf and 3IBR_*.out waveform sets were read successfully

limitations: Offline prediction only; PSCAD LVRT logic was not implemented.
abstract_trip_request does not mean BRK_DFIG opened.
VIBR1_2 is a candidate external POC voltage input; final PSCAD control wiring and filtering remain unconfirmed.

reference_comparison.comparison_note: replay trips because post-clear low-voltage recovery is included in the continuous event timer

confirmed_facts: No replayed 0.10 ohm duration case reached Vs <= 0.20 pu.
r0p10_d1p00, r0p10_d1p25, and r0p10_d1p50 produce abstract duration_exceeded trip_request under the minimum-voltage latch timer.
r0p10_d0p15, r0p10_d0p50, and r0p10_d0p75 recover before abstract trip_request.
engineering_choices: The timer monitors only after fault_start_s=2.0 for this replay to avoid startup low-voltage artifacts.
The continuous event remains active after fault clear until VIBR1_2 recovers to 0.90 pu or a trip latch occurs.
unresolved_pscad_integration_items: Confirm the live PSCAD signal/source that should feed the external LVRT block, instead of reading saved VIBR1_2 output.
Confirm voltage filtering, sample timing, and pu base for the future control input.
Define how abstract trip_request should drive BRK_DFIG or another disconnection path without conflicting with existing BRK_DFIG command/state logic.
Confirm reset/reclose policy and whether latch should persist for the entire simulation.
next_manual_step: In PSCAD GUI, identify the actual control-accessible voltage signal equivalent to VIBR1_2 and the safe insertion point for an external trip_request to BRK_DFIG, without wiring it yet.
